title: Higher-Order Functions
code:
twice :: (* -> *) -> * -> *
twice f x = f (f x)

compose :: (* -> **) -> (*** -> *) -> *** -> **
compose f g x = f (g x)

my_map :: (* -> **) -> [*] -> [**]
my_map f xs = [f x | x <- xs]

my_filter :: (* -> bool) -> [*] -> [*]
my_filter p xs = [x | x <- xs; p x]

my_foldr :: (* -> ** -> **) -> ** -> [*] -> **
my_foldr f z []     = z
my_foldr f z (x:xs) = f x (my_foldr f z xs)
description: Core higher-order functions implemented from scratch in Miranda. Uses `*`, `**`, `***` as distinct type variables. `my_map` and `my_filter` are defined with list comprehensions, while `my_foldr` uses pattern matching.
